text: Periodized carbohydrate restriction does not enhance endurance performance compared to training with normal high carbohydrate availability in endurance-trained athletes.
population: trained endurance athletes (VO2max ≥55 ml/kg/min females, ≥60 ml/kg/min males)

text: Training or recovery with low muscle glycogen stimulates mitochondrial biogenesis and favorable metabolic adaptations in trained athletes, but these cellular benefits do not translate to measurable performance gains.
Chronic carbohydrate restriction compromises training quality, particularly reducing intensities achievable during peak intervals.
Endurance athletes should maintain carbohydrate-rich diets to support optimal performance during competitions and intense training sessions.

If periodized carbohydrate restriction is used, it should be applied selectively to specific low-intensity sessions rather than as chronic restriction, to avoid compromising high-intensity training quality.
specific_value: ≥3 sessions per week of restricted CHO used in studies
